libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Disable Ehcache update checks in generated tests, for example with System.setProperty("net.sf.ehcache.skipUpdateCheck", "true") and Configuration.updateCheck(false) or XML updateCheck="false" for CacheManager instances. Use temporary directories for diskStore/diskPersistent tests. If exercising Bitronix lookup, create a temporary bitronix.properties with bitronix.tm.disableJmx=true and bitronix.tm.journal=null, set bitronix.tm.configuration before initializing Bitronix, then shut down TransactionManagerServices and clear the property after the test.

deterministic_setup.reason: ehcache-core 2.5.0 declares JTA as provided while transaction helpers, XA classes, and DefaultTransactionManagerLookup directly reference javax.transaction.* APIs.
DefaultTransactionManagerLookup has built-in Bitronix discovery/registration paths; Bitronix gives tests a concrete TransactionManager without requiring an application server.
Bitronix declares the JMS 1.1 API as optional, but its classes reference javax.jms types; supplying the spec jar avoids classpath/native-image analysis gaps when Bitronix is present.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8877 [Automation] native-image run fails for net.sf.ehcache:ehcache-core:2.5.0; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: ehcache-core also contains Hibernate, servlet/web, Terracotta clustering, and sizeof-agent integration classes with provided or external runtime requirements; generated tests should avoid those areas unless additional setup is intentionally added.
Terracotta clustered cache coverage would require a server/runtime setup and is not part of this preparation.
Update-checker networking can make tests flaky or unsuitable for native-image runs if not disabled.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Ehcache core is usable standalone, but meaningful coverage of its transaction/native failure area requires provided JTA APIs plus a concrete transaction manager; tests should also disable Ehcache's network update checker.
